Q1.answer: A desktop app that lets absolute novices build production web apps by chatting with Claude, offline, on their own machine.

Q2.answer: yes, with API key only; no separate user account
Q2.rationale: It is a single-user desktop app. The API key is the credential.

Q3.rationale: The Builder itself is free; the novice pays Anthropic directly for tokens.

Q5.answer: yes, central feature: Claude conducts the interview, ingests files, and orchestrates Claude Code

Q6.answer: yes, the orchestrator runs build phases that span minutes

Q7.answer: no inbound webhooks; only outbound calls to Anthropic, Vercel, GitHub

Q8.answer: yes, novice uploads PRDs, screenshots, schemas, transcripts, data samples

Q9.rationale: Novices may be in the EU/UK; the app processes their content. PII guard required.

Q10.answer: login-only equivalent: API key required to do anything beyond Welcome

Q12.answer: you choose; calm and minimal, dark and light modes

answer: First run: paste API key, create project
Recursive interview: chat to a complete spec
File ingestion: drop a PRD or screenshot, see it merged
Build: dashboard with live tail, ETA, drift surfacing
Deploy preview to Vercel and export to GitHub

Q16.answer: see spec.md section 3 Flows A through J for Given/When/Then

Q18.answer: just-users (single user per machine; no multi-tenancy)

Q19.answer: no paid tier; the Builder is free

Q20.answer: Tauri 2 desktop app, signed installers per platform

Q21.answer: very fast app launch and chat round-trip; see NFR budgets

Q22.answer: smoke + critical paths; full coverage on lib/ and orchestrator

Q23.answer: no telemetry by default; Sentry opt-in only after first build; novice content never leaves the machine except as Anthropic prompts

Q24.answer: Builder dev cost ceiling: 50 USD per development phase; novice cost ceiling: 5 GBP per build day, configurable

Q25.answer: Eat the kit's own dog food: the Builder is itself built using the Build Spec Kit, follows all rules, runs its own /recheck.

Q27.rationale: i18n deferred to a later phase per kit section 14.10

Q28.answer: yes: deleting a project, overwriting a non-empty folder, force-pushing to GitHub

uploaded_files.summary: The original onboarding-layer specification for the Builder
The Build Spec Kit defaults the Builder must follow and use
